regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-06-24] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 1.6% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.9% YoY | core CPI 2.58% | core PCE 2.78% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.3%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.51% | 10Y-2Y 0.34% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 49.8 | VIX 17.28

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ (research only; no execution path on this account).

1) MOVE-BY-MOVE READ. Both indices are mildly green on the day (ES +0.34%, NQ +0.60%) but red on the week (ES -0.74%, NQ -0.50%), so the cleanest framing is a modest intraday bounce inside a down week. The weekly weakness lines up with the tagged news: 'Tech Futures Plunge As Micron, Nvidia, Sandisk Dive' is the most direct catalyst for the NQ-side drawdown — semiconductor leadership got hit, which dragged the prior-week high (30,968) far above current spot (29,844). The Qualcomm/Modular ~$4bn deal narrative and the cluster of geopolitics headlines (US restricting Israeli action in Lebanon, Starlink-into-Iran claims, Iran banking cyberattack) keep a low-grade risk premium in the tape but are not clean directional drivers — they're noise-with-a-floor under volatility, not a thesis. Today's small green print is a VWAP-reclaim attempt: ES trades 7462.5 vs session VWAP 7451.39, NQ 29844.25 vs VWAP 29809.14 — both holding just above VWAP, which is the only constructive tell on the chart right now. RSI sits mid-40s on both (ES 44.8, NQ 45.0): neither oversold nor showing momentum — a genuinely neutral oscillator read. Both remain well above the 200-day (ES +7.5%, NQ +14.6%), so the larger uptrend structure is intact; this is a pullback inside an uptrend, not a regime break.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ. The 5-day profiles show price trading at the LOW end of where volume accumulated, which is the key context. ES POC sits at 7541.62 with value area 7435.38–7571.38; spot (7462.5) is in the lower third of value, below POC. NQ POC is 30592.39 with value area 29663.19–30725.13; spot (29844.25) is also below POC, near the lower edge of value. Translation: most recent business was done HIGHER, and price has slid to the bottom of the accepted range. The line in the sand for NQ is the value-area-low / prior-week-low cluster around 29,577–29,663 — that is the same level as the prior-day low (29,577.25) AND the prior-week low (29,577.25), a triple-confluence shelf. Hold there and 'pullback inside value' stays valid; lose it on volume and the profile says price is rejecting value to the downside. CAVEAT: this is volume-at-price (WHERE trade happened), NOT order-flow delta — I cannot see buy/sell aggression, so I will not over-read absorption at the lows.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME. Contemporaneous correlation is 0.945 with the lead-lag peak cleanly at lag 0 (0.925) and no meaningful predictive lag (best off-zero corr is a weak -0.118). Leader = NONE, divergence = FALSE. This is a clean BREADTH regime: ES and NQ are moving together, which makes the direction (whatever it is) more DURABLE and more trustworthy than a concentration/single-name-driven move. Practically, that means I can size a bit more normally on a directional read AND I should treat any ES/NQ disagreement today as a meaningful flag rather than noise — but right now there is no disagreement to exploit. Note the regime is an INPUT, not an edge.

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS. The 21-day horizon is event-heavy and front-loaded: PCE (Fed's preferred inflation) in 6 days (Jun 30, 8:30 ET), ISM Manufacturing in 7 days (Jul 1, 10:00 ET), Nonfarm Payrolls in 9 days (Jul 3, 8:30 ET — high impact), then CPI in 18 days (Jul 12). None hit today, so there's no event-day size reduction required for this session — but the calendar argues against building large swing exposure that would be carried blind into PCE/NFP. Reminder: PCE/ISM/NFP/CPI here are CADENCE ESTIMATES, not verified release dates — confirm against the official calendar before sizing into the window.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS. Meta-regime: pullback inside an intact uptrend, in a clean BREADTH tape, with price pinned at the LOWER edge of 5-day value and holding marginally above session VWAP. The bull case: VWAP reclaim + triple-confluence support at 29,577 + above 200-day. The bear case: down week, semis leading lower on real news, price below POC, mid-40s RSI with no momentum, and geopolitical headline risk. These roughly offset — so I will NOT manufacture a directional conviction. Bias: NEUTRAL-with-a-mild-long-lean while NQ holds above 29,577, ~52% the bounce continues toward POC/VWAP-up vs ~48% it fails — too close to call hard. The actionable structure: if I were to lean, it's a LONG-the-dip-back-above-VWAP idea, ~52%, INVALIDATED below 29,577 (loss of the prior-day/prior-week-low/value-area shelf flips the read to short-toward 29,613 lows and opens range expansion lower). Above 30,592 (NQ POC) the read inverts to constructive trend-resumption. CONFIDENCE IS LOW (42): news is mixed (clear tech-weakness driver but offsetting geopolitical noise) and while the lead-lag is clean, the price location is genuinely two-sided. HUMILITY NOTE: this scorecard is unproven — n=7 calls, regime accuracy only 0.333, still CALIBRATING and far short of the ~30–60 samples needed for the hit-rate to mean anything. Treat this as intelligence, not a validated edge.

big_move_headline.headline: Today's likeliest institutional move: SHORT EURUSD (P 70%, ~0.64% range, conf 41%).

ES=F.limitation: Volume profile = WHERE volume traded (volume-at-price). NOT order-flow delta (buy/sell aggression). True delta needs a CME order-flow feed (Databento/CQG).

NQ=F.limitation: Volume profile = WHERE volume traded (volume-at-price). NOT order-flow delta (buy/sell aggression). True delta needs a CME order-flow feed (Databento/CQG).
